mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal or UNRATE > 3.5% fails to correlate with 20d return deciles (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declines in policy rates (FEDFUNDS) signal stagflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of CPIAUCSL > 300 and FEDFUNDS < 5% fails to predict negative returns in 60%+ of cases or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP (GDPC1) followed by a rebound in housing starts (HOUST) signals early-stage economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1 contraction-HOUST rebound pattern fails to predict positive returns in 50%+ of cases or 60-day return deciles show no correlation with the signal (Spearman rho < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 103.0258 signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO < 103.0258 fails to predict negative 5d returns in 60%+ of cases or 5-day return deciles show no correlation with the signal (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market dynamics, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS trends (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer and business demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-HOUST growth combination fails to predict positive returns in 50%+ of cases or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 302.918 combined with policy rate increases (FEDFUNDS > 4.83%) signals inflationary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS tightening combination fails to predict negative returns in 60%+ of cases or 60-day return deciles show no correlation with the signal (Spearman rho < 0.1).
